narration: Import the argparse module to use for command-line argument parsing.
Define the main entry point using the if __name__ == '__main__' statement.
Add a positional argument named 'size' with type string and choices restricted to the keys of the sizes constant.
Set custom help text for the size argument to describe the pizza size.
Call parser.parse_args() to parse command-line arguments from sys.argv.
Add a positional argument named 'crust' with type string and choices restricted to the keys of the crusts constant.
Set custom help text for the crust argument to describe the pizza crust type.
Add an optional argument named 'extra_cheese' with a short form '-c' and action store_true to create a flag.
Set the destination for the extra cheese argument to 'cheese' to store the value in a variable named cheese.
Add an optional argument named 'extra_sauce' with a short form '-s' and action store_true to create a flag.
Add an optional argument named 'toppings' with a short form '-t' and nargs='+' to accept one or more string arguments.
Access pizza attributes from the namespace object using dot notation (e.g., order.size, order.crust).
Check if toppings exist in the namespace object before adding them to the pizza.
Access the cheese attribute from the namespace object to add extra cheese to the pizza.
Access the sauce attribute from the namespace object to add extra sauce to the pizza.
Store the output of the build_pizza function and print the final pizza message.
